prompt: Memory pool allocator returns overlapping memory blocks - test_alloc_no_overlap fails

turns.content:
<task>
Memory pool allocator returns overlapping memory blocks - test_alloc_no_overlap fails
</task>

arguments.reasoning: Line 26: `pool->offset = pool->offset & ~7` rounds DOWN to 8-byte alignment, not up. If offset is 5, this becomes 0, causing the next allocation to overlap with the previous one. It should round UP: `pool->offset = (pool->offset + 7) & ~7`.

arguments.summary: Found alignment bug at line 26: `offset & ~7` rounds DOWN to 8 bytes, causing overlap. Should round UP with `(offset + 7) & ~7`.

next_steps.description: Fix line 26: change `pool->offset & ~7` to `(pool->offset + 7) & ~7` to round up to next 8-byte boundary
Run make test to verify no overlap

turns.content:

arguments.summary: Fixed alignment: changed `offset & ~7` (rounds down) to `(offset + 7) & ~7` (rounds up) for proper 8-byte alignment. No more overlapping blocks. All 3 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed memory pool alignment bug in src/mempool.c line 26. `pool->offset & ~7` rounds DOWN causing overlapping blocks. Changed to `(pool->offset + 7) & ~7` which rounds UP to the next 8-byte boundary. All tests pass.
